_doc: Supplemental cell-line entries for workhorse mouse lines NOT covered by MCCA (the MCCA cohort is heavily KRAS-GEMM-biased and misses the carcinogen-induced / spontaneous tumour lines that dominate everyday mouse-tumour work). Each entry is hand-curated from primary literature, with the source paper PMID under `litCitation`. Same field shape as metadata.json; `dataSource: 'Literature'` flags provenance for the UI. Anyone re-processing MCCA shouldn't touch this file.

_schemaNotes.immuneProfile: Per-line immune characterisation. Sourced from Mosely 2017 (PMID 28768203) where covered; TISMO / individual papers otherwise. Fields: tmbCategory (high/medium/low), msiStatus (high/stable/unknown), immunePhenotype (hot / inflamed / variable / cold), icbResponse (responsive / partial / resistant / untested), notes (free-text), source.
_schemaNotes.ncitDisease: NCI Thesaurus disease label for the cell line, from Cellosaurus.
_schemaNotes.cautions: Important caveats from Cellosaurus comment-list (variant heterogeneity, mis-identification risk, etc.).

lines.name: MC38
lines.litCitation: Corbett 1975 (Cancer Res 35:2434, PMID 1212137); exome / immune profile in Mosely 2017 (Cell Reports, PMID 28768203); Cellosaurus CVCL_B288
lines.mouseModelDetailed: 1,2-Dimethylhydrazine (DMH) induced colon adenocarcinoma in a female C57BL/6 mouse (Corbett 1975).

immuneProfile.tmbDetail: Highest TMB among the carcinogen-induced syngeneic panel; ~800+ non-synonymous mutations (Mosely 2017).
immuneProfile.msiDetail: Microsatellite-unstable, defective MMR phenotype consistent with DMH-induced mucosal damage.
immuneProfile.immunePhenotypeDetail: T-cell inflamed; substantial CD8+ tumour infiltration relative to the cold lines (B16-F10, 4T1, LL/2).
immuneProfile.icbResponseDetail: Anti-PD-1 and anti-PD-L1 sensitive — the canonical positive-control syngeneic model for checkpoint-blockade efficacy studies.

lines.distributor: Available via collaborators / Kerafast / Memorial Sloan Kettering bank.
cautions: Kerafast (MC38-K) and Leiden University Medical Center (MC38-L) variants differ substantially in mutations and mRNA expression — note your source when comparing studies (PMID 36969213).
Historical confusion with the rat line 'MCA38' / 'isolate 406'; the mitochondrial sequence KM820832.1 deposited as MCA38 is actually rat (PMID 25434994).

lines.name: LL/2
lines.litCitation: Lewis 1951 (Bull Johns Hopkins Hosp 88:251); immune profile in Mosely 2017 (Cell Reports, PMID 28768203); Cellosaurus CVCL_4358
lines.ncitDisease: Malignant tumors of the mouse pulmonary system
lines.mouseModelDetailed: Spontaneously arising lung carcinoma in a C57BL/6 mouse (Lewis 1951). KRAS-mutant, TP53-mutant. Used as the canonical 'cold' / immune-excluded NSCLC syngeneic model and a standard negative-control line for ICB efficacy studies.
lines.metastasis: Highly metastatic to lung when injected intravenously; one of the most-used syngeneic metastasis models.

immuneProfile.tmbDetail: Low TMB relative to MC38 / CT26; consistent with the spontaneous low-mutator origin.
immuneProfile.immunePhenotypeDetail: Immune-excluded / cold tumour. Low CD8+ infiltration, high myeloid / MDSC content (Mosely 2017).
immuneProfile.icbResponseDetail: Anti-PD-1 / anti-PD-L1 monotherapy resistant — used as the canonical negative-control syngeneic line for checkpoint-blockade studies. Some combination strategies (anti-PD-L1 + chemotherapy / anti-CTLA-4) show partial efficacy.

cautions: Cellosaurus notes LL/2 could be identical to 3LL (CVCL_5653). The two designations refer to clonal derivatives of the same Lewis 1951 parent tumour; treat data across the two as comparable but not strictly interchangeable.
Sex of the originating mouse is not consistently reported in the primary literature — left as unknown rather than guess.

lines.name: Panc02
lines.litCitation: Corbett 1984 (Cancer Res 44:717, 3-methylcholanthrene-induced PDAC); immune profile in Mosely 2017 (Cell Reports, PMID 28768203); Cellosaurus CVCL_D627
lines.mouseModelDetailed: 3-methylcholanthrene-induced pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma in a male C57BL/6 mouse (Corbett 1984). The carcinogen-induced origin produces a higher mutational burden than KRAS-driven GEMM PDAC models — useful for studies where neoantigen load matters, but biologically distinct from human KRAS-PDAC.
lines.metastasis: Intraperitoneal injection gives peritoneal carcinomatosis with ascites and liver / lung micro-metastases.

immuneProfile.tmbDetail: Higher TMB than KRAS-GEMM pancreatic models (carcinogen-induced); intermediate between LL/2 and MC38.
immuneProfile.immunePhenotypeDetail: Variable across studies — orthotopic implantation produces stromal / immune-excluded tumours, while subcutaneous can be more inflamed. Lacks the dense fibrotic stroma of human PDAC.
immuneProfile.icbResponseDetail: Reports of partial monotherapy response to anti-PD-L1; widely used for combination ICB / vaccine studies rather than monotherapy efficacy.

cautions: Panc02 has been criticised as poorly representing human PDAC biology: it is more immunogenic than KRAS-driven GEMM PDAC, lacks the dense desmoplastic stroma, and behaves quite differently from KPC. Use as a 'permissive' PDAC syngeneic model rather than a faithful surrogate for KRAS-mutant disease.

lines.name: MOC1
lines.litCitation: Judd 2012 (Cancer Res 72:365, PMID 22086849); follow-up immune characterisation in Onken 2014 (Oral Oncol, PMID 24668645); Cellosaurus CVCL_ZD32
lines.ncitDisease: Squamous cell carcinoma of the mouse oral cavity
lines.mouseModelDetailed: Established by Judd et al. 2012 from a C57BL/6 mouse oral squamous cell carcinoma induced by 4-nitroquinoline-1-oxide (4-NQO). MOC1 is the immunogenic / T-cell inflamed sister line in the MOC1 vs MOC2 pair, used as the positive-control HNSCC model for ICB efficacy.
lines.metastasis: Locally invasive; limited spontaneous metastasis from orthotopic / buccal implantation.

immuneProfile.tmbDetail: High TMB consistent with 4-NQO-induced HNSCC; comparable to MC38.
immuneProfile.immunePhenotypeDetail: T-cell inflamed with substantial CD8+ infiltration; the immunogenic member of the MOC1/MOC2 pair.
immuneProfile.icbResponseDetail: Anti-PD-1 / anti-CTLA-4 responsive in monotherapy or combination — positive-control HNSCC syngeneic line.

lines.media: IMDM/Ham's F-12 (2:1) + 5% FBS + EGF + hydrocortisone + insulin (Judd 2012 keratinocyte cocktail).

lines.name: MOC2
lines.litCitation: Judd 2012 (Cancer Res 72:365, PMID 22086849); follow-up in Onken 2014 (Oral Oncol, PMID 24668645); Cellosaurus CVCL_ZD33
lines.ncitDisease: Squamous cell carcinoma of the mouse oral cavity
lines.mouseModelDetailed: Sister line to MOC1 from the same 4-NQO-induced cohort (Judd 2012). MOC2 is the aggressive, immune-excluded / ICB-resistant member of the pair — together MOC1/MOC2 are the canonical 'hot vs cold within one cancer type' contrast.
lines.metastasis: Aggressive locoregional invasion and lymph-node metastasis from orthotopic implantation — used as the metastatic HNSCC model.

immuneProfile.tmbDetail: Lower TMB than MOC1 despite the same 4-NQO carcinogen — explains part of the immune-cold phenotype.
immuneProfile.immunePhenotypeDetail: Immune-excluded / cold tumour with low CD8+ infiltration and high myeloid content; canonical resistant HNSCC model.
immuneProfile.icbResponseDetail: Anti-PD-1 / anti-CTLA-4 monotherapy resistant — used as the negative-control HNSCC syngeneic line and for combination-therapy escalation studies.

lines.media: IMDM/Ham's F-12 (2:1) + 5% FBS + EGF + hydrocortisone + insulin (Judd 2012 keratinocyte cocktail).
cautions: Cellosaurus annotates the host strain as 'C57BL/6 Cxcr3-/-' — the line was characterised in CXCR3-knockout hosts in some studies. Standard usage is in WT C57BL/6 recipients, but this is worth knowing when comparing CXCL9/10/11–CXCR3 axis findings across labs.

lines.name: ID8
lines.litCitation: Roby 2000 (Carcinogenesis 21:585, PMID 10753190); immune characterisation in Walton 2016 (Cancer Res, PMID 27235858) for the p53/Brca2-edited derivative; Cellosaurus CVCL_IU14
lines.morphologyDetailed: Spontaneously-transformed ovarian surface epithelial cells (Mouse Ovarian Surface Epithelial Cells = MOSEC).
lines.mouseModelDetailed: Adult female C57BL/6 ovarian surface epithelium serially passaged in vitro until spontaneous transformation (Roby 2000). The parental ID8 is p53-WT; the widely-used Walton derivatives carry CRISPR Trp53-/- and Trp53-/-Brca2-/- edits and behave quite differently — ID8-Trp53/Brca2-KO is responsive to PARPi + ICB while parental ID8 is largely resistant.
lines.metastasis: Intraperitoneal injection produces ovarian-cancer-like ascites and peritoneal carcinomatosis — the standard mouse high-grade serous ovarian cancer surrogate.

immuneProfile.tmbDetail: Low TMB — spontaneous in-vitro transformation, few somatic mutations.
immuneProfile.immunePhenotypeDetail: Immune-excluded / cold; large peritoneal disease burden with suppressive myeloid microenvironment.
immuneProfile.icbResponseDetail: Parental ID8 is anti-PD-1 / anti-PD-L1 monotherapy resistant. The CRISPR Trp53-/-Brca2-/- derivative becomes responsive to PARPi + ICB combinations — use the matching derivative for HRD / BRCA studies.

lines.distributor: Collaborator distribution; CRISPR derivatives via Walton lab.
cautions: The 'ID8' you receive may be the parental p53-WT line OR one of the Walton CRISPR derivatives (Trp53-/-, Trp53-/-Brca2-/-, etc.). These behave very differently in ICB and PARPi response — always confirm genotype with the depositor.

lines.name: TC-1
lines.litCitation: Lin 1996 (Cancer Res 56:21, PMID 8548765, Wu/Hung lab); Cellosaurus CVCL_4699
lines.mouseModelDetailed: Primary C57BL/6 lung epithelial cells co-transformed in vitro with HPV-16 E6/E7 and an activated c-Ha-ras oncogene (Lin et al. 1996). NOT a spontaneous cervical-cancer line — it is engineered lung epithelium that serves as a surrogate HPV-expressing tumour model. Standard for HPV-vaccine / E7-targeted immunotherapy studies.
lines.metastasis: Subcutaneous engraftment standard; limited spontaneous metastasis.

immuneProfile.tmbDetail: Low somatic TMB (in-vitro engineered, few accumulated mutations) — immunogenicity is driven by the foreign HPV-16 E6/E7 viral antigens, not by neoantigens.
immuneProfile.immunePhenotypeDetail: Tumour is rejected by mice immunised against E7 (canonical HPV-vaccine readout). Untreated TC-1 grows progressively with modest baseline T-cell infiltration.
immuneProfile.icbResponseDetail: ICB monotherapy gives partial efficacy; pairs strongly with E7-peptide vaccines or DNA vaccines — used to demonstrate vaccine + ICB synergy.

lines.media: RPMI-1640 + 10% FBS + L-glutamine + pen/strep + non-essential amino acids (typical).
cautions: Sex of the originating C57BL/6 donor not recorded in the primary literature — left as unknown.

Frequently labelled 'cervical cancer model' in vaccine papers — biologically it is engineered lung epithelium with HPV-16 oncogenes. Use as a model of HPV-driven tumour immunology, not as a cervical-cancer cell line.

lines.name: B16-F1
lines.litCitation: Fidler 1975 (Cancer Res 35:218, PMID 1109790; F1/F10 metastatic-variant series); Cellosaurus CVCL_0158
lines.mouseModelDetailed: Parental sub-line of the Fidler / Nicolson B16 metastatic-variant series (Fidler 1975). F1 = first round of in-vivo lung-colonising selection; F10 (CVCL_0159) is the more-metastatic descendant that dominates published syngeneic-melanoma work. Both are immune-cold / poorly-immunogenic.
lines.metastasis: Selected for lung-colonising potential; F10 (descendant) is the more metastatic variant. F1 is moderately metastatic.

immuneProfile.tmbDetail: Low-to-moderate TMB; spontaneous melanoma origin without strong mutational signatures.
immuneProfile.immunePhenotypeDetail: Classically immune-cold. Low baseline MHC-I expression (induceable with IFN-γ), low CD8+ infiltration — used as the prototypical poorly-immunogenic melanoma model.
immuneProfile.icbResponseDetail: Anti-PD-1 / anti-CTLA-4 monotherapy largely resistant (rare partial responders). Used as the negative-control melanoma syngeneic; pairs with cytokine therapy, adoptive T cells, or vaccine combinations for efficacy.

cautions: Most published 'B16 melanoma' work uses B16-F10 (CVCL_0159), not B16-F1. The two share an origin but F10 has been further selected for lung-colonising / metastatic potential and shows different growth kinetics. Confirm which sub-line your collaborators use.
